title: OSMM Journey Object
description: The orchestrated customer path AND its delivery configuration: the journey goal, the stages a customer moves through, the triggers that advance them, the sequencing/cadence, and the operational delivery logic that implements it. Spans the former Journey Strategy and Journey Configuration objects. Canonical schema for object_type 'journey'. The osmm-journey-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to.

journey_id.description: Stable id: 'JNY-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

journey_goal.description: The single outcome the journey drives (4.2). An outcome, not an activity.

journey_type.description: Optional governed motion. Use 'lifecycle' for an always-on journey spanning several motions.

stages.description: The ordered customer path (4.2). Array order is journey order.

objective.description: What this stage is trying to achieve.

entry_criteria.description: What moves a customer into this stage.

triggers.description: The triggering logic (4.5): the events that advance a customer and what each fires.

sequencing_logic.description: How stages/messages are sequenced — ordering, branching, pacing (4.5).

channels.description: Touchpoint channels used across the journey.

delivery_logic.description: Optional operational delivery/configuration notes (the former Journey Configuration, sub-process 6.3): platform flow shape, wait steps, audience-sync mechanics, branch conditions, message bindings. Distilled, not a platform export.

linked_campaign_strategy.description: Optional. The Campaign Strategy this journey serves — omit for always-on lifecycle journeys.

linked_personas.description: Persona ids this journey is designed around.

source.description: One line: provenance and approximate date.
